title: eupatorium stoechadifolium var. typicum
intro: Eupatorium stoechadifolium var. typicum, also known as White Snakeroot, is a perennial plant native to Eastern North America, belonging to the sunflower family (Asteraceae). Historically, the plant was used in herbal medicine for its purported medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which are attributed to its sesquiterpene lactone content. The plant is believed to offer health benefits such as reducing fever, alleviating rheumatism, and treating wounds. In herbal preparations, White Snakeroot can be consumed as a tea, infused in hot water or used as a tincture, although its use is not recommended without proper medical supervision due to potential toxicity and side effects.
